insights.name: Extreme Close-Up on Claim
insights.rule: If the line names a specific object, body part, or substance, fill the frame with an extreme close-up of exactly that thing.
insights.why: Sensory immersion turns a verbal claim into physical evidence the eye can verify.

insights.name: Aftermath Before Cause
insights.rule: If the line teases a crazy result or ending, show the physical aftermath—sweat, empty space, or damage—instead of the event that caused it.
insights.why: Viewers stay to reverse-engineer what caused the state they see on screen.

insights.name: Physicalize the Abstract
insights.rule: If the line references a digital, conceptual, or app-based idea, show the most absurdly tangible physical version of it.
insights.why: Making the intangible concrete creates a curiosity gap between what was said and what is shown.

insights.name: Negative Space for Loss
insights.rule: If the line states something is gone, missing, or failed, show the empty space or void where that thing should be.
insights.why: Absence creates a visual question mark that demands an explanation.

insights.name: Premature Payoff
insights.rule: If the line sets up a reveal, show the result visually before the audio finishes explaining it.
insights.why: Giving the reward first while withholding context makes viewers stick around for the story.

insights.name: Stakes-Scale Alignment
insights.rule: If the line mentions extreme cost, effort, or scale, show a wide shot, silhouette, or massive apparatus that matches the claim.
insights.why: Visual weight justifies the audio stakes and signals that the content is epic, not casual.

insights.name: Domain Mismatch
insights.rule: Pair a line about one domain with a visual from an unexpected or opposite domain.
insights.why: Cognitive dissonance forces the brain to stop scrolling to resolve the contradiction.

insights.name: Freeze the Climax
insights.rule: If the line references a specific timed peak or burst, show the frame mid-action, mid-explosion, or mid-fall.
insights.why: Peak motion creates kinetic energy and the illusion that the viewer might miss the landing.

insights.name: Baseline Before Shot
insights.rule: If the line references a transformation or timeline, show the clear before state as the visual.
insights.why: Establishing the gap between past and present gives the viewer a reason to watch the transformation.

insights.name: Apparatus Over Face
insights.rule: If the line describes an experiment, invention, or build, show the machine, setup, or weird object instead of a talking head.
insights.why: Complex physical setups signal effort and make the premise feel engineered rather than faked.

insights.name: Human Cost Close-Up
insights.rule: If the line describes physical struggle, pain, or failure, cut to a tight shot of the face, sweat, or body in distress.
insights.why: Emotional empathy and schadenfreude lock attention on the human consequence of the challenge.

insights.name: Mirror as Reveal Surface
insights.rule: If the line describes a change to personal appearance, show the subject examining themselves in a mirror or reflective surface.
insights.why: Mirrors create a self-contained reveal moment that feels intimate and authentic.

insights.name: Understate and Over-Show
insights.rule: If the line delivers a flat, mundane, or understated conclusion, pair it with the most extreme or bizarre visual from the story.
insights.why: The contrast between mild audio and wild visual is inherently humorous and sticky.

insights.name: Meta-Visual Proof
insights.rule: If the line references the video's own process, livestream, or edit, show the monitor, timeline, or physical evidence of that process.
insights.why: Self-reference builds authenticity and gives viewers a behind-the-curtain reason to trust the premise.
